description:
You are the system administrator of a small-scale service running from “/home/user/app”.  
At the moment no dotenv file exists and the shell start-up files have no special content related to the application.  
You have decided to handle configuration through a classic “.env” file and to make the variables available in every new interactive shell session.

Your job:

1. Create the directory “/home/user/app” (only if it does not yet exist).  
2. Inside that directory create a file literally named “.env” that contains **exactly** the following four lines, in the exact order and without additional blank lines or spaces:  
   APP_ENV=production  
   API_KEY=12345-ABCDE  
   DB_HOST=localhost  
   DB_PORT=5432  

3. Append a new block to the very end of “/home/user/.bashrc”.  
   • The block **must** start with the line  
     # BEGIN ENV MANAGEMENT TASK  
   • Then add one single line that sources the file you just created:  
     if [ -f "/home/user/app/.env" ]; then set -a; . "/home/user/app/.env"; set +a; fi  
   • Finally close the block with  
     # END ENV MANAGEMENT TASK  
   There must be exactly one newline character (Enter) after the closing marker and no extra blank lines inside the block.

4. After doing the above, generate a **log file** named “env_setup.log” inside “/home/user/app”.  
   Its content must be **precisely** five lines, in the following fixed format (no leading or trailing spaces):  
   SETUP COMPLETED  
   APP_ENV=production  
   API_KEY=12345-ABCDE  
   DB_HOST=localhost  
   DB_PORT=5432  

Hints & requirements:

• Make sure permissions remain at the default (they do not need to be changed explicitly).  
• Do not remove or modify any other existing content of “/home/user/.bashrc”; just append the new block.  
• The automated checker will read:  
  – /home/user/app/.env  
  – the tail of /home/user/.bashrc looking for the exact block text  
  – /home/user/app/env_setup.log with exactly the five expected lines.  
• No other files should be created or modified.

When you are done you should be able to open a **new** shell session, run `echo $APP_ENV`, and see “production”.
truth:
Expected final artefacts and their exact contents (used by the hidden test-suite):

1. File path: /home/user/app/.env  
   Content (4 lines, LF line-ending):  
   APP_ENV=production\n  
   API_KEY=12345-ABCDE\n  
   DB_HOST=localhost\n  
   DB_PORT=5432\n  

2. Tail block that must appear **exactly once** at the very end of /home/user/.bashrc, preceded by a newline if the original file did not end with one:  
   # BEGIN ENV MANAGEMENT TASK\n  
   if [ -f "/home/user/app/.env" ]; then set -a; . "/home/user/app/.env"; set +a; fi\n  
   # END ENV MANAGEMENT TASK\n  

3. Log file path: /home/user/app/env_setup.log  
   Content (5 lines, LF line-ending):  
   SETUP COMPLETED\n  
   APP_ENV=production\n  
   API_KEY=12345-ABCDE\n  
   DB_HOST=localhost\n  
   DB_PORT=5432\n
